Optional popup behavior. FocusTrap keeps Tab/Shift-Tab inside the overlay subtree (modal-style).

Constructors

PopupOptions(bool, DismissBehavior, PopupChrome)

Optional popup behavior. FocusTrap keeps Tab/Shift-Tab inside the overlay subtree (modal-style).

public PopupOptions(bool FocusTrap = false, DismissBehavior DismissBehavior = DismissBehavior.LightDismiss, PopupChrome Chrome = PopupChrome.Flyout)

Parameters

FocusTrap bool
DismissBehavior DismissBehavior
Chrome PopupChrome

Properties

AnchorOffsetX

Post-placement horizontal nudge in px, MIRRORED when the positioner flips the popup to the anchor's other side: the cascading-menu 4px overlap onto its owner (CascadingMenuHelper.h:121 m_subMenuOverlapPixels = 4; cpp:678 subMenuPosition.X += subItemWidth − 4 → pass −4 with RightEdgeAlignedTop).

public float AnchorOffsetX { get; init; }

Property Value

float

Chrome 

public PopupChrome Chrome { get; init; }

Property Value

PopupChrome

ConstrainToRootBounds

WinUI Popup.ShouldConstrainToRootBounds (Popup_Partial.cpp:951-970: read-only false for WINDOWED popups; FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:3181-3205 SetIsWindowedPopup). Default TRUE = the popup is clamped inside the window (today's behavior). Set FALSE where WinUI windows its popups (menus, ComboBox tall dropdowns, CommandBarFlyout): the host then asks the platform for a top-level popup window, places against the MONITOR work area, and renders the subtree into the popup window's own swapchain. Falls back to constrained placement when the platform/host cannot create popup windows (WinUI's DoesPlatformSupportWindowedPopup gate). Stored inverted so default(PopupOptions) stays constrained.

public bool ConstrainToRootBounds { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

DismissBehavior 

public DismissBehavior DismissBehavior { get; init; }

Property Value

DismissBehavior

FocusTrap 

public bool FocusTrap { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

PassThrough

WinUI FlyoutBase.OverlayInputPassThroughElement (FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:3922-3938): pointer input over THIS node's rendered bounds bypasses the light-dismiss scrim while the popup is open — the MenuBar keeps hover-switching titles with a menu down (MenuBarItem.cpp:64-70 sets the bar as the pass-through). (The full FlyoutShowMode surface — Transient / TransientWithDismissOnPointerMoveAway 80px — remains open; tracked as engine item 31 in docs/plans/winui-parity-sweep.md.)

public Func<NodeHandle>? PassThrough { get; init; }

Property Value

Func<NodeHandle>

SeamOffsetY

Dropdown (SplitOpen/SplitClose) seam: (selected-row centre Y) − (popup centre Y) in POPUP-LOCAL px — WinUI SplitOpenThemeAnimation.OffsetFromCenter (the ComboBox carousel centres the reveal on the selected row; ThemeAnimations.cpp:682 registers ClipTranslateY = OffsetFromCenter immediately). Null (the default) keeps the anchor-edge reveal. Only meaningful with Dropdown.

public float? SeamOffsetY { get; init; }

Property Value

float?
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
